name: Agreni
tagline: curriculum designer, researcher, and educator working at the intersection of equity, power, and learning.
bio: I am an educator and curriculum designer with nearly a decade of experience building learning environments that are rigorous, humane, and grounded in the realities of the students and teachers who inhabit them. My work spans schools, nonprofits, and ed-tech organizations across India and New York City — from designing English textbooks used in 300 partner schools, to teaching 6th grade in a Delhi public school, to researching how systems of surveillance shape the lives of female students. I hold a Master of Arts in Curriculum and Teaching from Teachers College, Columbia University, where my thesis examined the physical and emotional violence inflicted on female students in Indian schools through a Foucauldian lens. I am committed to work that takes both the scholarship and the student seriously.
